title: This Croc Cousin Started on All Fours, Then Walked Like a T-Rex!
summary:
Have you ever seen a puppy that walks on all fours, but its grown-up version walks on two legs? That's exactly what scientists just discovered about a very old reptile!

Meet Sonselasuchus cedrus (say: sawn-SAY-la-SOOK-us SEE-drus). This creature was about the size of a poodle—25 inches tall—and lived over 200 million years ago. It belonged to a group called shuvosaurids, which are relatives of today's crocodiles. But it didn't look like a croc. It had a toothless beak, big eye sockets, and hollow bones, kind of like a dinosaur.

Here's the wild part: when it was young, it walked on four legs. As it grew up, its back legs got much longer and stronger, so it started walking on two legs! Scientists figured this out by measuring the leg bones of many fossils found at Petrified Forest National Park in Arizona.

"We think these creatures started out on four legs and then walked on two as they grew up," said lead researcher Elliott Armour Smith. "This is particularly peculiar."

Even though it looks like some dinosaurs, scientists say these features evolved separately—like two different artists drawing the same cool design without copying each other. How amazing is that?
why_it_matters: This story shows that animals can change in surprising ways as they grow, just like you change from a baby to a kid.

keywords.explanation: A reptile that lived over 200 million years ago, about the size of a poodle.
A group of reptiles that are relatives of today's crocodiles.
Large reptiles that live in water and have long jaws.
A mouth part without teeth, like a bird's beak.
The pair of legs at the rear of the animal.
The preserved remains of ancient plants or animals.

questions.question: What group of reptiles does Sonselasuchus belong to?
When it was young, how did Sonselasuchus walk?
What happened to its back legs as it grew up?
What did the scientists use to learn about the reptile's walking?

background_read: Many animals change how they move as they grow up. For example, human babies crawl before they walk.
The Petrified Forest National Park in Arizona is famous for its ancient fossils and colorful petrified wood.
Crocodiles today walk on four legs, but their ancient relatives came in many different shapes and sizes.
Article_Structure: WHO: Sonselasuchus cedrus, a small reptile related to crocodiles
WHAT: It walked on four legs when young and switched to two legs as an adult
WHERE: Petrified Forest National Park in Arizona
WHY: Scientists measured leg bones from many fossils to discover this change

perspectives.description: They think this growth pattern is very unusual and helps us understand how reptiles evolved.
It sounds strange for an animal to switch from four legs to two, but nature has many surprises.
This discovery gives a fun example of how fossils reveal secrets about ancient life.
